data.title: Trevi Therapeutics outlines $204M cash runway into 2029 as Phase III programs advance following positive CORAL data
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Trevi Therapeutics (TRVI) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Jennifer L. Good, CEO, described the first half of the year as a major inflection point for Trevi, highlighting positive data from the CORAL trial in idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) chronic cough and the RIVER trial in refractory chronic cough (RCC). She stated, “As a result of this strong data, we were able to raise approximately $115 million in capital for total cash and investments at the end of June of approximately $204 million. This gives us strong runway to execute against several important clinical milestones in each of our programs.”
  • The CORAL trial met its primary endpoint with a statistically significant reduction in 24-hour cough frequency across all dose groups. Full data confirmed secondary endpoints were consistent with the top line, and the Leicester Cough Questionnaire (LCQ) showed marked improvements: “For the 54-milligram and 108 milligram BID doses, there was a statistically significant 3.7 and 3.4 point increase, respectively... at week 6.”
  • Plans include initiating a Phase III program for cough in IPF in the first half of next year, a study for non-IPF interstitial lung diseases (ILD), and a Phase IIb parallel arm study in RCC. The company is preparing for FDA meetings to align on Phase III protocols and plans to request an end of Phase II FDA meeting in the fourth quarter.
  • CFO Lisa Delfini stated, “For the second quarter of 2025, we reported a net loss of $12.3 million compared to a net loss of $12.4 million for the same quarter in 2024.” She noted R&D expenses decreased to $9.4 million due to reduced costs from completed trials, while G&A expenses rose to $4.3 million driven by increased professional fees and personnel. She emphasized, “Our cash and investment balance gives us cash runway into 2029 and enables us to fund 2 Phase III trials of Haduvio for the treatment of chronic cough in patients with IPF, our planned trial for chronic cough in patients with non-IPF-ILD, our next trial in patients with RCC, the Phase I work Jennifer discussed, and it enables pre-commercial planning activities.”

Outlook

  • Jennifer L. Good said Trevi will provide more detailed guidance when each trial is initiated. The company expects to request an end of Phase II FDA meeting in the fourth quarter to discuss alignment on the IPF cough Phase III program and NDA-enabling work. The Phase III program for cough in IPF and the next study in non-IPF ILD are targeted for initiation in the first half of next year.
  • Management reported readiness to launch multiple clinical programs concurrently, emphasizing that the expanded cash position supports advancement into pivotal trials and commercial planning.

Financial Results

  • The company reported a net loss of $12.3 million for Q2 2025 and noted R&D expenses decreased to $9.4 million, primarily due to lower costs from completed clinical trials. G&A expenses increased to $4.3 million, with approximately $500,000 attributed to SOX Section 404(b) compliance as market cap grows.
  • Trevi's cash and investments reached $204 million at June 30, 2025, following a successful public offering post-CORAL trial data release that raised approximately $115 million. This increase provides a runway into 2029 and funds for planned Phase III and other clinical activities.

Q&A

  • Faisal Ali Khurshid, Leerink Partners, asked about the respiratory depression study and potential risks. Chief Development Officer James V. Cassella responded that the study is informative and required by FDA, with no expected risks, and that data will be ready for the end of Phase II meeting.
  • Jason Dorr, Oppenheimer, inquired about key topics for the upcoming FDA meeting and plans for the non-IPF ILD trial. Cassella explained that the meeting will focus on Phase III readiness, protocol specifics, and safety database requirements, and that the non-IPF ILD study will broaden the patient pool with endpoints similar to IPF.
  • Annabel Eva Samimy, Stifel, questioned the rationale for a parallel design in the non-IPF ILD study and tolerability of Haduvio. Cassella confirmed a broader inclusion approach and noted, “There's no real changes that we're thinking about going forward,” citing strong tolerability and quality of life data.
  • Alexa Rose Deemer, Cantor Fitzgerald, asked about dose selection for Phase III. Cassella highlighted the 54-milligram BID dose as the key candidate, pending FDA discussion.
  • Serge D. Belanger, Needham & Company, requested more context on LCQ data and operational expenses. Cassella described robust domain improvements and Simon noted LCQ’s relevance for reimbursement. Delfini confirmed OpEx should remain near Q2 levels for the remainder of 2025.
  • Additional questions addressed competitive landscape, trial designs, manufacturing, commercialization strategy, and expected medical conference presentations. Management consistently signaled confidence in study design, supply chain readiness, and US focus for commercialization, with potential partnering in Europe or Japan.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on clinical trial progress, regulatory alignment, and competitive positioning, frequently expressing interest in risk management and trial design evolution. Their tone was generally neutral to slightly positive, with congratulatory remarks and forward-looking queries.
  • Management maintained a confident and informative tone, especially regarding cash runway, clinical results, and regulatory planning. Cassella and Good repeatedly emphasized data strength and preparedness for next steps, using phrases like “we are well positioned to execute our strategy.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management are more positive, reflecting the successful CORAL data and strengthened financial position. Management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism about Phase II results to confident execution of pivotal programs.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from awaiting key data to outlining clear plans for multiple pivotal and supportive trials, following positive CORAL and RIVER data.
  • Strategic focus expanded to include parallel trial advancement in IPF, non-IPF ILD, and RCC. The company’s financial position improved markedly, with cash and investments rising to $204 million from $103.3 million in Q1.
  • Analysts’ focus evolved from uncertainty around data readouts to implementation and regulatory strategies. Management’s sentiment is more confident, with detailed discussion of trial design and regulatory planning, while analysts remain attentive but less risk-oriented.
  • Key metrics showed improved cash position and slightly reduced net loss, with operational expense projections stabilizing.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges discussed included the need for FDA alignment on Phase III programs, regulatory requirements for drug-drug interaction and respiratory safety studies, and operational scaling for new trials.
  • Management’s mitigation strategy is to engage proactively with the FDA, conduct required Phase I studies, and maintain readiness on manufacturing and supply. No new or unexpected adverse safety signals were reported.
  • Analysts probed the impact of competitive developments, potential regulatory delays, and commercial strategy in ex-US markets, but management indicated these are not expected to impact current programs.

Final Takeaway

Trevi Therapeutics management emphasized that strong clinical data from the CORAL and RIVER trials, combined with a $204 million cash position, has positioned the company to advance multiple pivotal trials and pre-commercial activities with confidence. The company is targeting initiation of Phase III programs in IPF and non-IPF ILD cough in the first half of next year, and expects continued regulatory engagement and operational readiness to drive value creation through 2029.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: VTEX targets 6–9% FX-neutral subscription revenue growth for Q3 2025 while raising margin outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: VTEX (VTEX) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Geraldo do Carmo Thomaz, Co-CEO, emphasized that “VTEX continued to deliver resilient performance driven by disciplined execution and relevant progress on our global expansion.” He announced that the company raised its non-GAAP income from operations and free cash flow guidance by over 10% and highlighted U.S. and Europe as "growing twice as fast as the overall company." The CEO pointed to an expansion of the partnership with Whirlpool in the U.S., the launch of KitchenAid’s website, and the addition of enterprise customers across the U.S. and Europe as key milestones.
  • Thomaz detailed that subscription revenue reached $57.2 million, representing an 11% year-over-year increase on an FX-neutral basis. However, he noted this was within guidance in U.S. dollars but below on an FX-neutral basis. He cited a reversal of recovery in Argentina and a mix shift in Brazil toward larger customers with lower implied take rates.
  • Strategic priorities cited included B2B commerce, retail media, omnichannel, and agentic commerce. The CEO highlighted the introduction of a re-architected B2B buyer portal, the positioning of VTEX Ads as a monetization engine with a new Globo partnership, and innovations like AI-powered semantic search and agentic commerce features.
  • Ricardo Camatta Sodre, CFO, stated, “In the second quarter of 2025, GMV reached $4.8 billion, growing 9% year-over-year in U.S. dollars and 14% on an FX-neutral basis.” He noted a 6% increase in subscription revenue in U.S. dollars and an 11% increase on an FX-neutral basis. Sodre explained, “Our non-GAAP subscription gross margin reached 80% in Q2 2025, up 180 basis points year-over-year from 78% in Q2 2024.” He also remarked on the company’s disciplined execution and ability to maintain profitability and strong cash generation.

Outlook

  • For Q3 2025, VTEX is targeting FX-neutral year-over-year subscription revenue growth in the range of 6% to 9%, implying a range of $57.5 million to $59.0 million.
  • For the full year 2025, the company is targeting FX-neutral subscription revenue growth of 9% to 12%, translating to $233 million to $239 million based on July's average FX rate.
  • VTEX raised its full-year 2025 outlook for non-GAAP income from operations and free cash flow margins to the high teens, with Sodre noting an increase in non-GAAP income from operations and free cash flow guidance by more than 10% in dollar terms.

Financial Results

  • Subscription revenue for Q2 2025 was $57.2 million, up from $54.0 million in Q2 2024. GMV reached $4.8 billion, with 9% year-over-year growth in U.S. dollars and 14% on an FX-neutral basis.
  • Non-GAAP income from operations increased by 46% in FX-neutral terms to $8.5 million, reflecting a 14.4% margin. Total gross margin rose to 77%, up 350 basis points year-over-year.
  • Free cash flow for the quarter was $7.1 million, an improvement from $3.0 million a year ago. VTEX repurchased 0.8 million shares at an average price of $4.82 per share and authorized a new repurchase program of up to $40 million.

Q&A

  • Marcelo Peev dos Santos, JPMorgan: Asked about the decline in guidance for GMV and new subscriptions, and margin improvements. Ricardo Camatta Sodre explained, “The large majority of the FX-neutral impact comes from the Argentina and Brazil GMV effect, with a much smaller portion coming from the softer overall market demand and the few isolated contract cancellations.” He added that margin improvements stemmed from AI support automation and ecosystem maturity.
  • Lucca R. Brendim, Bank of America: Inquired about delays in onboarding large U.S. and European clients and competitive dynamics. Thomaz responded, "On the implementation cycle, we are not seeing any change, significant change... the project is undergoing, we don't see any kind of shortening or enlargement of the implementation cycle.” He stressed growing momentum in international markets.
  • Maria Clara Infantozzi, Itaú: Asked about Argentina’s performance and potential consumption deceleration in Brazil. Sodre described Argentina as a "significant drag" with a reversal to double-digit negative GMV growth in Q2, and indicated that guidance for Brazil assumes some deceleration for the second half, factoring high interest rates.
  • Unidentified Analyst: Questioned whether market volatility would affect the B2B and retail media strategy. Thomaz said, “All these macroeconomic shifts are not informing any new actions on these 2 ventures.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern about declining guidance and regional volatility, especially in Argentina and Brazil, with a slightly negative to neutral tone.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, highlighting “resilient operational profitability” and “strength of our execution.” During Q&A, management responses were measured, offering detailed explanations for guidance changes and margin improvements.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management exhibited more caution and focus on near-term headwinds, with management emphasizing long-term strategy and operational resilience.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for FX-neutral subscription revenue growth was lowered from 12.5–15.5% (Q1 call) to 6–9% for Q3 and 9–12% for the full year, reflecting more cautious expectations amid market headwinds.
  • Management’s focus shifted further toward profitability and operational leverage, increasing margin outlook despite reduced revenue guidance.
  • Analysts’ questions centered more on regional volatility, client onboarding pace, and the impact of macroeconomic challenges, compared to a previously more optimistic tone.
  • Strategic priorities such as global expansion, B2B, and retail media remained consistent, but management highlighted the immediate impact of Argentina’s volatility and Brazil’s mix shift more prominently.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified Argentina’s GMV reversal and ongoing volatility as key challenges, along with a mix shift in Brazil toward larger clients with lower take rates.
  • Sodre cited “softer overall market migrations and a few isolated contract cancellations tied to high-risk implementations” as additional headwinds.
  • Guidance was revised to reflect these factors, with management emphasizing efforts to work closely with clients and maintain margin discipline.

Final Takeaway

VTEX’s Q2 2025 earnings call demonstrated disciplined execution amid macroeconomic pressure, with management raising margin guidance even as subscription revenue growth targets were revised downward. The company highlighted resilient profitability, robust international expansion, and continued investment in B2B and retail media as key drivers for future growth, while acknowledging the impact of volatility in Argentina and a shifting client mix in Brazil. Management reiterated confidence in the long-term outlook, underlining scalable operations and a focus on delivering value to customers and shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Bain Capital backed Heartflow surges on public debut
data.description:
Heartflow (NASDAQ:HTFL), a MedTech firm backed by private equity firms including Bain Capital, climbed ~50% on its first trading session as a public company on Friday.

On Thursday evening, the Mountain View, California-based company priced the IPO of nearly 16.7M shares at $19.00 apiece, seeking to raise $316.7M in gross proceeds.

Heartflow (NASDAQ:HTFL), whose AI-enabled products target the management and diagnosis of heart disease, initially sought to offer 12.5M shares at $15–$17 apiece before boosting it to 16.7M shares priced at $17–$18 per share.

HTFL opened at $28.00 at about 12:50 PM ET on Nasdaq, up ~47%, to quickly peak at $29.70, implying a ~56% gain at 1:00 PM ET.

As for financials, the developer of the Heartflow Platform has incurred significant losses since its launch in 2007.

In 2024, the company recorded $128.8M in revenue, indicating ~44% YoY growth, while its net loss widened ~1% to $96.4M. Based on preliminary unaudited data, Heartflow (NASDAQ:HTFL) projects $42.9M - $43.4M in revenue for Q2 2025 with 38% YoY - 40% YoY growth.

data.title: Tech Voices: Intel CEO vs. Trump, Open AI profitability, Microsoft
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

-Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) CEO Lip-Bu Tan responded to accusations made by President Trump on Thursday that he was "highly conflicted" and needed to resign from his post.

"There has been a lot of misinformation circulating about my past roles at Walden International and Cadence Design Systems (NASDAQ:CDNS). I want to be absolutely clear: Over 40+ years in the industry, I’ve built relationships around the world and across our diverse ecosystem – and I have always operated within the highest legal and ethical standards," Tan said in an employee memo posted on Intel's website.

"We are engaging with the Administration to address the matters that have been raised and ensure they have the facts. I fully share the President’s commitment to advancing U.S. national and economic security," Tan continued.

"The Board is fully supportive of the work we are doing to transform our company, innovate for our customers, and execute with discipline – and we are making progress," Tan added.

-OpenAI CEO Sam Altman told CNBC on Friday that his company needs to prioritize growth and investing in its technology "for a long time,"  even if that means delaying profitability. 

“As long as we’re on this very distinct curve of the model getting better and better, I think the rational thing to do is to just be willing to run the loss for quite a while,” Altman said in an interview with CNBC on Friday.

OpenAI released its latest AI model, GPT-5, on Thursday.

-Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), which holds a major stake in OpenAI, said that it has already launched GPT-5 across all its platforms, including Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot, GitHub Copilot and Azure AI Foundry. 

"It's the most capable model yet from our partners at OpenAI, bringing powerful new advances in reasoning, coding, and chat, all trained on Azure," said Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella in a post on X. 

"The pace of progress is only accelerating, and I can’t wait to see what developers, enterprises, and consumers will do with this latest breakthrough," Nadella added.

data.title: Wheaton signals 40% production growth by 2029 while maintaining $1B liquidity
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Wheaton Precious Metals Corp. (WPM) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Randy V. J. Smallwood, CEO, announced the appointment of Haytham Hodaly as President and Curt Bernardi as Executive Vice President, Strategy and General Counsel. He stated, “As we enter a new phase of transformative growth, the leadership of Haytham and Curt will play -- will be pivotal in driving our strategy forward.”
  • Smallwood highlighted, “Wheaton delivered another outstanding quarter, achieving record revenue, adjusted net earnings and operating cash flow for both the second quarter and the first half of 2025. This performance underscores the effectiveness of our streaming business model in leveraging rising commodity prices while maintaining strong margins.”
  • The company recorded production of 159,000 gold equivalent ounces this quarter, with key assets Salobo and Peñasquito contributing significantly. Wheaton remains “well positioned to achieve our 2025 production guidance of 600,000 to 670,000 gold equivalent ounces.”
  • Smallwood emphasized progress on growth strategy, noting, “Blackwater announced commercial production and Goose successfully delivered its first gold pour during the quarter, a strong indicator that our catalyst-rich year is progressing as planned.”
  • Wheaton’s cash position at quarter end was over $1 billion, with a $2 billion undrawn revolving credit facility. Smallwood stated, “Our forecasted organic growth profile of 40% production growth by 2029 enables us to pursue opportunities that best complement our growth trajectory.”
  • Vincent Lau, CFO, reported, “Strong commodity prices, coupled with solid production, led to record quarterly revenue of $503 million, an increase of 68% compared to last year.” Lau added, “Net earnings increased by 139% from prior year to $292 million, while adjusted net earnings increased by 91% to reach a record $286 million. Operating cash flow increased to $450 million, a 77% increase from last year.”

Outlook

  • Wheaton’s production outlook for 2025 remains unchanged, with a target of 600,000 to 670,000 gold equivalent ounces. Management reiterated, “We believe we will remain well on track to achieve our annual production guidance.”
  • Salobo production is forecast to remain steady through 2025, with Antamina and Constancia expected to increase output in the second half due to higher grades and throughput. Mineral Park, Goose, Platreef, and Aljustrel are forecast to contribute in the second half of 2025.
  • Management reiterated the “forecasted organic growth profile of 40% production growth by 2029.”

Financial Results

  • Quarterly revenue was $503 million, driven by a 32% increase in commodity prices and a 28% increase in sales volumes compared to the prior year.
  • Gold accounted for 65% of revenue, silver 33%, and the remainder from palladium and cobalt.
  • Net earnings were $292 million, with adjusted net earnings at $286 million. Operating cash flow reached $450 million.
  • Upfront cash payments for streams totaled $347 million, with significant allocations to Koné, Salobo, Kurmuk, and Cangrejos. Net cash outflows for the quarter were $80 million, resulting in a cash balance of $1 billion at June 30.

Q&A

  • Matthew Murphy, BMO Capital Markets, asked about Wheaton’s stance on deals versus organic growth. Haytham Henry Hodaly responded, “We focus on accretive transactions that will maintain our strong security structure... We are probably sitting between 12 and 15 opportunities that we're looking at right now. I would say 2/3 of those are development stage opportunities, 1/3 are probably operating and there's also some M&A opportunities in there as well.”
  • Brian MacArthur, Raymond James, inquired about U.S. gold tariffs and contract flexibility. Vincent Lau replied, “We're insulated from any of the tariffs. We take delivery of credits of gold and silver in mainly London. So we're not subject to any of these tariffs. So at minimum, we would achieve the LME price.”
  • Tanya M. Jakusconek, Scotiabank, questioned deal terms and guidance. Hodaly stated, “It's definitely more competitive, not restrictive. We're still seeing opportunities coming in daily and bidding on opportunities on a regular basis. We'll probably bid on more opportunities this year and larger opportunities than we have in any other year in the last 10 years.”
  • Daniel Edward Major, UBS, asked about expected cash tax payments and pipeline composition. Lau confirmed a $112 million expected payment in 2026 for the 2024 period. Hodaly noted, “I would say is probably about 2/3 of those are still diversified and about 1/3 would be precious metals from a precious metals company.”
  • Cosmos Chiu, CIBC, asked about sales versus production and Blackwater Phase 2 impact. Lau explained Q2 sales benefited from a drawdown in PBND and expects inventory to increase for the rest of 2025. G. Wesley Carson said acceleration of Blackwater Phase 2 is not currently included in long-term guidance.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment, with several congratulatory remarks on executive promotions and performance, and focused questions on growth opportunities, deal competitiveness, and production guidance.
  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly referencing a robust portfolio, strong liquidity, and an active pipeline. Phrases like “We believe we will remain well on track” and “We are definitely insulated from it” indicated confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained optimistic, but with heightened emphasis on deal competitiveness and increased activity in the pipeline, reflecting a slightly more proactive posture.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter features new executive appointments, notably promoting Hodaly to President, signaling a focus on leadership continuity.
  • Revenue, net earnings, and cash flow hit new records, with stronger growth rates than in Q1.
  • Guidance remains unchanged at 600,000 to 670,000 gold equivalent ounces for 2025, consistent with Q1.
  • Analysts’ questions this quarter increasingly centered on deal environment competitiveness, new project acceleration, and the impact of external factors such as tariffs, whereas Q1 focused more on production ramp-up and payment structures.
  • Management’s tone was consistently confident, with more detailed discussion about active deal flow and the company’s ability to deploy capital.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management addressed the competitive deal environment, noting, “It's definitely more competitive, not restrictive.”
  • Uncertainty about U.S. gold tariffs was discussed, but management emphasized insulation from direct impacts.
  • Analyst questions highlighted the timing and structure of future tax payments, with management providing clarity on expected cash tax outflows and pipeline composition.
  • Sales versus production timing variability was also noted as a factor that management monitors closely but cannot fully predict.

Final Takeaway

Wheaton Precious Metals delivered record financial and operational results in Q2 2025, maintaining its annual production guidance and reiterating a 40% production growth target by 2029. The company remains highly liquid with over $1 billion in cash, a robust pipeline of streaming opportunities, and a disciplined approach to capital deployment, all underpinned by recent executive promotions and a confident outlook for sustained growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Stereotaxis reiterates double-digit revenue growth outlook for 2025 as new catheter launches drive commercial momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Stereotaxis (STXS) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO David Leo Fischel described 2025 as “a pivotal milestone-rich year in which we advance a broad portfolio of strategic innovations to market and demonstrate their initial commercial impact.” He stated that results are “in line with the guidance provided at the start of this year.” Four strategic pillars were highlighted: making the robot more accessible, building an ecosystem of proprietary catheters, expanding into new clinical indications, and establishing a digital backbone for connectivity and AI in the cath lab.
  • Fischel reported the completion of the first commercial GenesisX system, with installation expected at the initial European customer, and anticipated “full launch in Europe and the U.S.” later in the year following regulatory milestones. He said, “We expect to demonstrate GenesisX working in daily clinical use in 1 or 2 hospitals still this year, upon which we should be ready for full launch in Europe and the U.S.”
  • He noted that the MAGiC ablation catheter contributed “approximately a couple of hundred thousand dollars in revenue from initial European customers during the second quarter,” with initial use at about 30% of EU accounts. U.S. regulatory approval for MAGiC is still expected later this year, with FDA clearance of the MAGiC Sweep mapping catheter already achieved.
  • Fischel called the FDA clearance of MAGiC Sweep “reflective of a major strategic transformation underway,” noting it is the company’s first catheter FDA clearance in nearly 20 years.
  • Additional proprietary catheters and wires are in regulatory review, with the Imagine catheter and digital surgery platform Synchrony both expected to receive approvals soon.
  • CFO Kimberly R. Peery stated, “Revenue for the second quarter of 2025 totaled $8.8 million, growth of 95% from $4.5 million in the prior year second quarter and growth of 18% sequentially compared to $7.5 million in the first quarter of 2025.”
  • Peery reported gross margin at 52%, operating expenses of $8.6 million, an operating loss of $4 million, and cash and equivalents of $7 million with no debt at quarter-end. She also announced a $12.5 million direct financing, with $8.5 million closed in July and the remainder expected within four months, resulting in “$18.8 million in cash and no debt.”

Outlook

  • Fischel reiterated guidance of “double-digit revenue growth for the full year 2025 with system revenue in any given quarter fluctuating between approximately $2 million to $3 million and recurring revenue scaling to approximately $7 million in the fourth quarter.”
  • He indicated, “Our revenue expectations assume only modest contribution from GenesisX and no system revenue from China.”
  • Fischel emphasized, “We expect to launch our new technologies with a balanced focus on accelerating growth while also ensuring improved margins, earnings accretion and achievement of profitability.”

Financial Results

  • Peery reported system revenue of $3 million and recurring revenue of $5.8 million for the quarter. She attributed recurring revenue growth to Map-iT catheters and initial MAGiC sales in Europe.
  • The gross margin for recurring revenue was 68%, while system gross margin stood at 22%. Peery explained, “Gross margins remain impacted by acquisition-related accounting that temporarily reduces disposable margin and by fixed overhead allocated over low system production levels.”
  • Adjusted operating expenses were $6 million, with a decline attributed partly to an employee retention tax credit and reduced R&D spending.
  • Adjusted operating loss was $1.4 million, and adjusted net loss was $1.3 million.

Q&A

  • Adam Carl Maeder, Piper Sandler: Asked about the use of proceeds from the recent financing and commercial strategy, including sales force expansion. Fischel responded that the company’s commercial team numbers around 40 globally and will shift toward “having 1 clinical rep per hospital rather than our current model where we have about 1 clinical rep for every 3 or 4 hospitals.” He added, “We will be reinvesting a lot of the incremental gross profit and operating profit from these catheters back into the sales team for that growth.”
  • Maeder: Inquired about MAGiC RF adoption in Europe and the status of MAGiC Sweep. Fischel said, “MAGiC in Europe, we talked about approximately 30% of our European accounts have now started to use MAGiC in procedures. That's up from about 20% when we talked last quarter.” He confirmed usage spans a broad range of procedure types.
  • Frank James Takkinen, Lake Street Capital Markets: Sought details on the FDA process for MAGiC and revenue contribution potential of Sweep. Fischel stated the company is working through questions on “sterility, biocompatibility and manufacturing practices” and remains confident in second-half approval. He explained, “High-density mapping catheters are typically priced north of $2,000 per procedure…high-density mapping is probably used in the majority…of the procedures that we see on our robots.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive and inquisitive tone, focusing on commercial execution, sales force expansion, and regulatory timelines. There were no skeptical or negative exchanges.
  • Management’s tone in prepared remarks was confident, emphasizing “pivotal” progress and commercial momentum. In Q&A, Fischel provided detailed, upbeat responses regarding operational plans and market adoption, using phrases such as “I believe you’ve seen pretty nicely,” and “we can do that in an accretive way.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remained confident but with greater emphasis on the transformational commercial impact of new product launches.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter featured updates on several regulatory milestones achieved, including FDA clearance of MAGiC Sweep and CE Mark for MAGiC, compared to ongoing regulatory processes in the prior quarter.
  • Revenue grew from $7.5 million in Q1 to $8.8 million in Q2; system revenue and recurring revenue both increased.
  • MAGiC adoption in Europe advanced from 20% to 30% of accounts.
  • Management reiterated double-digit growth guidance, consistent with Q1, but expressed greater confidence in leveraging new products for commercial scale.
  • Analyst questions shifted from pipeline and product feedback to sales force scaling and commercial execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Fischel acknowledged ongoing administrative challenges in Europe, describing the process as “an administrative grind.”
  • Peery highlighted gross margin pressures from acquisition-related accounting and low system production levels.
  • Management noted that revenue guidance assumes only “modest contribution from GenesisX and no system revenue from China.”
  • FDA approval timelines and regulatory interactions remain a critical dependency for U.S. product launches.

Final Takeaway

Stereotaxis management highlighted 2025 as a year of major innovation milestones, with successful launches of new robotic and catheter platforms already beginning to impact commercial results. The company reiterated its double-digit revenue growth outlook, noting stronger financial flexibility following a capital raise, and outlined plans for sales force expansion and further product launches as regulatory approvals are secured. Management emphasized that these achievements set the stage for accelerated growth and profitability as Stereotaxis transitions from early launches to broader market penetration.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Blend Labs targets $190M RPO and 43% consumer banking growth while advancing AI and platform strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Blend Labs, Inc. (BLND) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Nima Ghamsari, Co-Founder, Chairman, CEO & Head of Blend, stated, "We've now posted 4 quarters of year-over-year total revenue growth and 4 quarters of non-GAAP operating profitability." Ghamsari highlighted a leadership transition, thanking outgoing Head of Finance & Administration Amir Jafari. He noted strong sales momentum in Q2 with 23 new or expanded deals, double the previous quarter, including three net new logos in the independent mortgage bank (IMB) vertical and a record $190 million in remaining performance obligations (RPO). Ghamsari emphasized, "Our customer base always comes first," reporting a 70% decline in churn notices year-over-year and zero churn notices received in 2025 to date.
  • Ghamsari outlined strategic focus on expanding market share, increasing take rate with existing customers, and growing the consumer banking business to diversify revenue. He reported that consumer banking represented 36% of total revenue, up from 28% a year ago, with year-over-year growth of 43% in that segment. He also previewed the launch of an AI tool, saying, "Blend is currently piloting a new AI tool that sits across documents, data and origination guidelines."
  • Amir Jafari, Head of Finance & Administration, stated, "Total revenue in the second quarter of 2025 was $31.5 million, ahead of the midpoint of our guidance and up 10% year-over-year." Jafari confirmed consumer banking suite revenue of $11.4 million and a 3% decrease in mortgage suite revenue to $18 million. He noted a non-GAAP gross margin of 76% and a non-GAAP operating margin of 15%. Jafari discussed the expected sale of Title365 to Covius and the strategic transition to a software-first model.

Outlook

  • Management expects third quarter total revenue between $31.5 million and $33.5 million, with the midpoint representing a 2% year-over-year decline. Non-GAAP operating income is expected to be between $3 million and $4.5 million. Operating expenses for 2025 are anticipated to be in the range of $85 million to $90 million, with potential for actual expenses to come in below that range depending on market conditions. "We expect third quarter EVPFL to be approximately $85 to $86, and we expect to exit 2025 near the mid- to upper 80s," Jafari stated.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q2 2025 was $31.5 million, reflecting a 10% year-over-year increase, driven by 43% growth in consumer banking suite revenue. Mortgage suite revenue decreased 3% to $18 million. Non-GAAP gross profit was $24 million, with a non-GAAP gross margin of 76%, and non-GAAP operating income was $4.7 million. Free cash flow for the quarter was negative $9 million. Blend reported approximately $93.3 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities as of June 30, 2025. EVPFL for the quarter was $88. RPO reached a record $190 million, up from $158 million in Q1. Blend repurchased 1.3 million shares year-to-date, with $20.9 million remaining under the repurchase authorization.

Q&A

  • Dylan Tyler Becker, William Blair: Asked about the impact of rate volatility and the momentum in the home equity product. Ghamsari responded that while rate movements affect customer volumes, Blend is insulated by the growing home equity business, stating, "That's helping insulate us from the things that are out of our control like rate movements."
  • Becker: Inquired about the recovery pace for EVPFL as new customers ramp and Rapid Refi adoption increases. Jafari explained, "The expansion for us on EVPFL will come from...the ramp of the mortgage solution...and then the adoption of Close...Rapid Refi...will be the second component that allows us to...get EVPFL back to a growth rate."
  • Ryan John Tomasello, KBW: Sought details on the mix of new logos versus expansions among the 23 deals and IMB wins. Ghamsari indicated many IMB wins are competitive takeaways and attributed success to Blend's innovation and the dedicated IMB business unit. He did not provide a breakdown of new logos versus expansions.
  • Tomasello: Queried about the Rule of 40 target. Jafari said, "We're not in a position to make any changes yet."
  • Aaron Jacob Kimson, Citizens: Asked about the positioning of vertical software in an AI-driven industry. Ghamsari emphasized that vertical software enables rapid ROI and that AI will drive operational efficiencies for Blend's customers.
  • Kimson: Requested clarification on the home equity component in consumer banking growth. Jafari attributed the increase to seasonal factors and the success of Rapid Home Equity, along with contributions from deposits, credit cards, and auto.
  • Pallav Saini, Canaccord: Asked about investment needed for AI opportunities. Ghamsari noted, "We're very early in our AI journey...it's making us more efficient as an organization using the AI tools internally."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a generally positive tone, focusing on sales momentum, product adoption, and technology investments. Questions indicated interest in growth resilience and business model shifts.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing stability, growth, and innovation. In Q&A, management was responsive and highlighted customer-focused metrics and product strategy. Ghamsari described the stabilization of Blend's customer base as "quite a feat for any software company."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone from both analysts and management remains positive, with increased focus on the execution of strategic initiatives and growth in consumer banking.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q3 revenue is slightly below Q2, with a projected year-over-year decline, versus prior guidance that anticipated year-over-year growth. Operating expenses guidance remains consistent but may be revised downward. Consumer banking suite revenue continues to grow rapidly, with a shift in revenue mix. RPO increased from $158 million in Q1 to $190 million in Q2. EVPFL declined to $88 from $93, with management citing near-term headwinds but reaffirming expectations for recovery.
  • Management's confidence in product innovation, AI, and customer retention remains strong, with a more pronounced focus on market share gains and expanded product offerings compared to Q1. Analysts continue to probe for growth drivers and effects of strategic shifts, echoing themes of margin improvement and product expansion.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged near-term headwinds for EVPFL related to a large strategic IMB deal with lower upfront pricing. Jafari stated, "We have another near-term headwind that we expect to impact EVPFL for the rest of 2025."
  • The company identified continued mortgage market pressure and potential seasonality in market volumes as factors impacting financial outlook. Blend is "actively making adjustments to the business in response to ongoing pressure in the mortgage market."
  • Analysts raised questions about market volatility, rate changes, and the pace of recovery in high-margin products, as well as investment levels required for AI initiatives.

Final Takeaway

Blend Labs, Inc. management emphasized four consecutive quarters of year-over-year revenue growth and non-GAAP operating profitability, while highlighting strategic wins in expanding market share, customer retention, and product innovation. The company projects continued growth in consumer banking and ongoing investments in AI, with a record $190 million in remaining performance obligations and strong sales momentum. Management remains vigilant about near-term headwinds in mortgage pricing and is actively optimizing its business model to drive long-term value for customers and shareholders.
